recent_calls.summary: The user called to inquire about pricing and details for a plumbing conversion from a tub to a shower, including questions about licensing, permits, and VA HISA grant eligibility. The agent collected the user's contact information and noted the service address as Berkeley, Missouri, and arranged for a team member to follow up the next business day. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided feedback preferring to speak directly with a person.

The user, Predrag, called to report an issue with submitting an online form for air conditioning service. The agent documented the problem and informed the user that a representative will call back on the next business day since live transfers were unavailable after hours. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further assistance requested.

The user called to schedule a cooling service in Illinois. The agent confirmed the service type and informed the user that plumbing services are not offered in the area. The call ended abruptly after the user repeated their request several times without further response from the agent.

The user, Miranda Campschrader, called to report an issue with ice buildup on her air conditioner and requested service as soon as possible. The agent collected her contact details and service address, confirmed the service request, and informed her that the team would follow up. Miranda declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The call was very brief, with the user only saying "Two for" before hanging up. The agent introduced themselves but did not get a chance to assist the user further.
The user called to inquire about payment consent at the time of service. The agent was unable to access billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

The user called to reach the estimator who visited their house and requested to be connected to a live estimator. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate estimator. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.

Michael called to schedule an air conditioner repair through American Home Shield but did not have a dispatch number. The agent collected all necessary details and informed him that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him soon to confirm the appointment. Michael declined information about maintenance programs and expressed some frustration about previous response times before the call was transferred to a live agent.
The user called and requested to speak to Nicole. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to the appropriate person.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The user called and requested to speak to a human agent. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Dave from American Home Shield, called and requested to speak with a live agent. The agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user called to schedule a service order and provided their ZIP code. However, the call ended abruptly when the user expressed frustration and hung up. The agent was unable to complete the service request.

The user initially provided their ZIP code and expressed a desire to schedule an appointment, but the call was disrupted by repeated interruptions and the user expressing frustration with the AI agent. The call ended abruptly without resolution.

The user called to inquire about the status of a technician scheduled to fix their furnace, expressing concern about the delay. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user called to inquire about the timing of a repair appointment, expressing frustration about a delay after being told they were next. The agent acknowledged the frustration and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about service availability but provided ZIP codes that are not in the agent's service area. The user confirmed they only have one ZIP code, and the agent informed them that service is not available there. The call ended without further action.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

Patrick Wadlow called to check if the technician was still scheduled for 2 PM. The agent could not find the appointment in the system and offered to transfer Patrick to a live representative, which he accepted. The call was then successfully transferred to another agent.
The user requested to speak with an operator, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

The user called to inquire about office help hiring. The agent informed the user that they handle service requests but will forward the interest to the appropriate team and collected the user's contact information. The call ended politely with no further issues.

The user called to follow up on a missed call from CG and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The agent acknowledged the issue and promptly transferred the call. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

Al from American Home Shield called to follow up on the status of a work order with dispatch number 43500659. The agent noted the request and assured that the team would check the status and contact authorization using the info on file. The call ended with confirmation that the team will follow up accordingly.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

The user, Robin, called to follow up on a recent service and requested to speak with a customer service representative. The agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user inquired about the wait time for technicians to arrive at their home. The agent promptly attempted to transfer the call to a live agent who could provide further assistance. The call was successfully transferred to another agent.
The user requested to speak with a live representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to confirm an appointment but the agent was unable to find it in the system. The user requested to be transferred to a live representative for further assistance, and the call was successfully transferred.

The user called to dispute a $125 charge and expressed intent to cancel the service, mentioning they have someone coming out today. The agent apologized for not having billing access and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.

The user, Margaret, requested to speak with a live representative due to an issue related to her asthma and the need for assistance within 24 hours. The AI agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and the user responding with a single word before hanging up. No further conversation or assistance took place.
The user requested to speak with Nicole at Budget, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate person successfully.

The user, Sharon Adkins, called to confirm an appointment for June 12th. The agent could not find the appointment in the system and offered to leave a message or transfer to a live representative. The user requested a live representative, and the call was successfully transferred.

The user called to inquire about the estimated time of arrival for a morning appointment. The agent confirmed the appointment details and found the appointment scheduled between 2 and 3 PM. Due to the user's frustration with previous service, the call was transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called Budget seeking water-related service but was informed that plumbing services are not offered. Due to communication difficulties, the call was transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user reported that their unit stopped cooling after a recent service visit and requested urgent service to fix the completely shut off unit. The agent confirmed the service address and contact details, and assured the user that the team would be notified immediately. The call ended with the user expressing thanks.

The user called Budget and provided their ZIP code, then requested parts and asked to speak to a specific operator named CG. The call ended shortly after the user's request without further resolution.
The user called to return a missed phone call and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to confirm that they are not automatically re-enrolled or charged for the Budget Savings Club furnace tune-up service for another year, as they did not want to continue the service. The agent informed the user that they do not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

Deborah Singleton called to set up a service request for her cooling system, which is making a humming noise. She provided her contact details and requested service for the same day but had difficulty confirming her exact street address. The call was transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

Angel from American Home Shield called to inquire about the status of a contractor report for dispatch number 47242859 related to Yolanda Griffin. The agent, Sophie, informed Angel that she would pass the request to the appropriate team for follow-up. The call ended with the agent confirming the request would be forwarded and offering further assistance if needed.

Michael Holliday called to schedule a repair appointment for his air conditioner, which is not blowing cold air, under his American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the details and informed Michael that a technician would contact him soon. Michael declined additional information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback on the call experience.

The user called back after receiving a call from the company, expecting to schedule an appointment for an AC inspection. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, James Patton, called mistakenly thinking it was a repair call. The agent confirmed no appointment was scheduled and offered to transfer him to a live representative, which was done successfully. The user expressed frustration about not knowing if a technician was coming soon.
The user requested to speak to Amy, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

Matt called to report that his air conditioner, which was checked last week with no issues found, is still not cooling properly and runs constantly. The agent confirmed his contact details and service address, assured him the team would follow up promptly, and ended the call after addressing his concerns.
The user called after receiving a missed call from the company. The agent acknowledged the call and transferred the user to a live agent for further assistance.
